sanskrit: सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः । मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ॥
hindiTranslation: मेरा आश्रय लेनेवाला भक्त सदा सब कर्म करता हुआ भी मेरी कृपासे शाश्वत अविनाशी पदको प्राप्त हो जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: A devotee who takes refuge in me, while always doing all actions, attains the eternal, imperishable state through my grace.
essence: Continuous action and complete refuge can lead to the same unchanging state.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Keep doing all your work, but rely on Krishna rather than your own control. That surrender leads to an eternal, unchanging state.
simpleInsight: You can stay active without feeling alone in the burden. Grace makes the path feel supported.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is being shown the final shape of devotion. Krishna says that even while a person keeps acting in the world, taking refuge in him leads to the imperishable state beyond fear and loss.
modernRelevance: You send the hard message, sign the painful form, or walk into the difficult room anyway. The body acts; the grip on control loosens.
translationLiteral: सर्वकर्माणि (all actions) / अपि (even) / सदा (always) / कुर्वाणः (doing) / मद्व्यपाश्रयः (taking refuge in me) / मत्प्रसादात् (by my grace) / अवाप्नोति (attains) / शाश्वतम् (eternal) / पदम् (state) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
insight: It shifts action from self-reliance to surrendered participation, where effort continues but ownership of the result is released.

quoteVariants.text: Do every duty. Refuge in the divine brings the imperishable state.
Act always. Rest in the divine.
The work continues; grace carries the rest.
